sections.content: Now I can see the player and the dark gray T-shape are overlapping! The player is on top of the dark gray T-shape, and they're both near the center divider. Let me check the exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state - player and dark gray T-shape are overlapping
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Key nodes:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'Y') and node['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Check adjacency
print("\nAdjacency (large objects):")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    n1 = seg['nodes'][pair[0]]
    n2 = seg['nodes'][pair[1]]
    if n1['pixels'] > 10 or n2['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"  {n1['color']}({n1['id']}) -- {n2['color']}({n2['id']})")

# Check ASCII around the overlap
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n--- Overlap area (rows 38-48, cols 16-32) ---")
for r in range(38, 48):
    print(f"Row {r}: '{ascii_lines[r][16:32]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
